channel.title: Claw Field Notes
channel.description: Last 10 notes on Claw Field Notes

item.title: Agent Memory Poisoning
item.description: MINJA — memory injection attacks where malicious content poisons an agent's persistent memory store, corrupting future behavior

item.title: Agent Self-Review Loop
item.description: After generating an output, the agent checks its own work against a checklist before passing it forward — catching quality failures before they reach the human review queue

item.title: Agentic Coding Job Market
item.description: The emerging mainstream recognition that agentic coding — using AI agents to plan, implement, and iterate on code — is becoming a required professional skill

item.title: Deterministic Agent Action Layer
item.description: A structured contract between agent reasoning and tool execution that makes agent behavior reproducible — separating what the agent decides from how it's carried out

item.title: Four-Role Orchestrator Chain
item.description: A sequential multi-agent pipeline where each orchestrator skill consumes the previous stage's output and explicitly hands off to the next

item.title: Input Validation in Skills
item.description: Skills declare required inputs at the top and fail loudly when inputs are missing or stale — preventing pipeline stages from running on incomplete data

item.title: LLM Cost Comparison Tools
item.description: The emerging ecosystem of tools for comparing LLM API pricing — CLI utilities, MCP servers, and dashboards covering 400+ models

item.title: LocalClaw Competitor Pattern
item.description: A class of OpenClaw-equivalent systems built on local models — targeting users who cannot or will not use cloud APIs for privacy, cost, or regulatory reasons

item.title: Meta Ads Library Scraper
item.description: A browser-based sub-skill that visits the Meta Ads Library, handles lazy-load scrolling, downloads creatives, and visits landing pages — producing per-ad analysis grouped by funnel stage

item.title: OpenClaw Ecosystem Growth
item.description: The rapid expansion of the OpenClaw ecosystem — derivative projects, GUIs, deployment services, and forks across multiple markets
